title: Chapter 4: The Heirloom
content: The Liang family held a memorial ceremony every month.

Ren learned this on day five of his new life as the world's most strategically valuable parasite. The ceremony honored Liang Tianming's late wife—Liang Xue and Liang Feng's mother—who had died under circumstances no one discussed. The entire household attended. The Patriarch presided. And the family's bloodline artifacts were brought out and displayed on an altar that predated the current dynasty.

This was Ren's first ceremony. He hadn't been invited to the previous ones—when you're the despised son-in-law, you're included in family events the way a stain is included in a carpet. Technically present, but everyone wishes you weren't.
This time was different. Liang Xue had told him to attend. Verbally. In front of witnesses.
*"You've proven you have a brain. Now prove you have manners."*
Reading between the lines: she wanted him visible, in a controlled environment, where she could observe how he operated outside the library. She was testing him.
The War God approved. Tests went both ways.

The ceremony hall was in the estate's oldest wing—a structure that predated the modern compound by three hundred years, preserved like a fossil in amber. Carved wooden pillars, paper lanterns, an altar of black stone that seemed to drink the candlelight. The air smelled of incense and something else—something electric and ancient that made Ren's skin prickle.
The Salvation System painted a translucent overlay across his vision:

`ARRAY DENSITY: Extreme`
`LEVIATHAN-CLASS ARRAY DETECTED`
`ORIGIN: Pre-dynastic (estimated 800+ years)`
`FUNCTION: Bloodline attunement + dimensional stability`
`WARNING: Do NOT touch the altar. Do NOT approach the inner door.`
The inner door. Set into the back wall of the ceremony hall, behind the altar—a door of black iron that seemed to absorb light. The Forbidden Zone's entrance.
Ren made a mental note of its exact position, dimensions, and the array signatures radiating from its frame. Then he filed the information away and turned his attention to the ceremony.

The Liang family members filed in by rank. Patriarch first—Liang Tianming, a man who moved like smoke and watched like a hawk. Sixty-three years old, silver-haired, with a face carved from jade and eyes that missed nothing. He didn't look at Ren. That was worse than a glare. It meant Ren wasn't significant enough to register as a threat.
Yet.

Behind the Patriarch came the core bloodline members—Xue, Feng, and a handful of cousins and uncles Ren hadn't met. They wore formal hanfu in the family colors of deep green and silver. The jade pendant at Liang Xue's throat caught the candlelight and threw prismatic fragments across the walls.
Ren's breath caught.
The Salvation System blazed:

`🚨 CRITICAL ARRAY DETECTION`
`ARTIFACT: Liang Bloodline Key Fragment (Jade Pendant)`
`CLASSIFICATION: Portal Control Component — Node 3 of 7`
`ACTIVATION STATE: Dormant (requires bloodline channeler)`
`DANGER: This artifact is one of seven components required to open the dimensional portal. Currently 3 of 7 assembled.`
`PROXIMITY ALERT: Artifact responding to your causal key!`
The pendant was glowing.
Faint—so faint that no one without array sensitivity could see it—but the green jade was *pulsing*, a slow heartbeat of light in rhythm with Ren's breathing.
He looked away instantly. Controlled his breathing. Counted heartbeats until the system's alert dimmed.
*Nobody saw that.* The ceremony was focused on the Patriarch and the altar. Xue was standing with her eyes closed, hands folded. Feng was fidgeting. The cousins were bored.
But the pendant had *responded* to him. The casual key—the legal, familial recognition that made him a "member" of the Liang bloodline despite having no blood connection—had triggered the artifact.
Why?
The system processed:
`ANALYSIS: Your causal key (spousal recognition) creates a partial resonance with bloodline artifacts. This resonance is normally too weak to trigger activation. However...`
`HYPOTHESIS: The Salvation System's array-hacking capability may be amplifying the resonance. You are effectively "loud" to bloodline artifacts in a way that normal spouses would not be.`

`IMPLICATION: You can detect and interact with portal components. But this also means the components can detect YOU. If the Patriarch's surveillance arrays are monitoring artifact responses, your proximity may have been flagged.`
*Wonderful.* Not only was the end of the world hanging around his wife's neck, but it was *talking* to him in public.

The ceremony continued. Incense spiraled upward. The Patriarch's voice droned ancient words—words that weren't Chinese, weren't any human language, but made the array on the altar hum with power. Feng squirmed beside his sister, and Ren caught the moment when his hand twitched toward his own chest—a scratching motion, like something beneath his skin was crawling.
*Stage 2.* Ren was certain now. The spore had progressed. The infection was accelerating.
In the previous timeline, Feng had reached Stage 5 in fourteen months. If the rate was similar—
*Thirteen months until he becomes a vessel. Maybe less.*

The ceremony ended. The family filed out. Ren stood with the non-bloodline hangers-on—the spouses and partners who attended out of obligation. He watched Liang Xue's retreating back, the jade pendant warm against her throat, and felt the weight of 1092 days pressing against his chest.
He needed to get closer to that pendant. He needed to understand its function, its vulnerabilities, whether it could be removed or disabled without collapsing the firewall.
He needed to get closer to Liang Xue.
Which was exactly the problem.
That night, Ren lay in the guest quarters—a small room in the east wing, far from the family's private apartments. The bed was comfortable. The silence was not.
The Salvation System ran diagnostics while he stared at the ceiling.

`ARRAY COMPONENT STATUS (Portal Control):`
`Node 1: Altar Stone (Secured in Forbidden Zone) — ACCESS DENIED`
`Node 2: Patriarch's Seal (On Liang Tianming's person) — ACCESS DENIED`
`Node 3: Jade Pendant (On Liang Xue's person) — PROXIMITY ACCESSIBLE`
`Node 4: UNKNOWN — Location unconfirmed`
`Node 5: UNKNOWN — Location unconfirmed`
`Node 6: UNKNOWN — Location unconfirmed`
`Node 7: UNKNOWN — Location unconfirmed`
Three of seven nodes identified. One accessible. The others unknown—possibly distributed among family members, possibly hidden, possibly not yet assembled.

The portal wasn't complete. That was the good news. The bad news was that the Patriarch was methodically collecting components, and three of seven was already terrifying. In the previous timeline, the full array had activated with all seven nodes in place, powered by a blood sacrifice that had cracked reality open like an egg.
*I need to find the other four nodes before he does.*
But finding them meant access. Access meant trust. Trust meant time. And time was the one thing he was running out of.
His phone buzzed. The unknown number. Third time.
This time, there was a message:
*"The jade pendant is Node 3. There are seven nodes total. The Patriarch has four. You've seen one. Would you like to know where the other two are?"*
Ren stared at the screen for a long time.
Then he typed: *"Who are you?"*
The response was immediate:
*"Someone who died in the future you remember. Someone who came back differently."*
The phone went dark.

Ren lay in the darkness, heart pounding, and thought about enemies who hadn't revealed themselves. About allies who might be enemies. About the thin golden thread of a marriage that was the only thing keeping the world intact.
And about the jade pendant at Liang Xue's throat—the key to the apocalypse, worn by the one woman he couldn't afford to lose and couldn't afford to trust.
One thousand ninety-two days.
